name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 20.04.2026

texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 20.04.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 20.04.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on April 17, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 69.06 lakh ha as compared to 66.14 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 30.64 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.
texts.text:  As on April 17, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 69.06 lakh ha as compared to 66.14 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 30.64 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 17.04.2026, 81.69% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 97% area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Gram  (98%),  Urdbean  (98%)  &  Moong  (82%);  98.41%  area  of  Oilseeds majorly  in  Rapeseed  & Mustard  (100%); 81.58%  area of  Shri  Anna cum  Coarse Cereals  majorly in Jowar  (91.44%),  47.89%  area  of  Rice  and  75.00%  area  of  Wheat  has  been  harvested  for  rabi  crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 17.04.2026, 81.69% of area has been harvested for Rabi crops all over India. Approximately 97% area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Gram  (98%),  Urdbean  (98%)  &  Moong  (82%);  98.41%  area  of  Oilseeds majorly  in  Rapeseed  & Mustard  (100%); 81.58%  area of  Shri  Anna cum  Coarse Cereals  majorly in Jowar  (91.44%),  47.89%  area  of  Rice  and  75.00%  area  of  Wheat  has  been  harvested  for  rabi  crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Northwest  India, East  India, Chhattisgarh, Konkan-Goa, coastal Karnataka, many parts of Kerala, Northern parts of coastal Andhra Pradesh and some parts of Tamil Nadu.
texts.text: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Northwest  India, East  India, Chhattisgarh, Konkan-Goa, coastal Karnataka, many parts of Kerala, Northern parts of coastal Andhra Pradesh and some parts of Tamil Nadu.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  April  19  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  6% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 19%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 5%; (iii) Central India: 25%; and South Peninsula: (-) 16%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  April  19  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  6% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 19%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 5%; (iii) Central India: 25%; and South Peninsula: (-) 16%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect Western Himalayan region from 23 rd April.
texts.text:  A fresh Western Disturbance is likely to affect Western Himalayan region from 23 rd April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  over  Arunachal  Pradesh  and  Assam  on  20 th and 21 st April.
texts.text:  Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  over  Arunachal  Pradesh  and  Assam  on  20 th and 21 st April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Scattered to Fairly widespread light to moderate rainfall/snowfall with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds likely over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad during 23rd-25th and Isolated to Scattered over Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand during the period.
texts.text:  Scattered to Fairly widespread light to moderate rainfall/snowfall with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds likely over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-Baltistan-Muzaffarabad during 23rd-25th and Isolated to Scattered over Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand during the period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Thunderstorms activity likely to continue during the week  along east coast of India, as well as coastal areas of Kerala and Karnataka.
texts.text:  Thunderstorms activity likely to continue during the week  along east coast of India, as well as coastal areas of Kerala and Karnataka.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Widespread rainfall activity likely over Northeast India, Western Himalayan Region and Isolated rainfall activity over Gujarat and Western parts of Peninsular India..
texts.text:  Widespread rainfall activity likely over Northeast India, Western Himalayan Region and Isolated rainfall activity over Gujarat and Western parts of Peninsular India..

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat  wave  conditions  very  likely  in  isolated  pockets  of  West  Madhya  Pradesh  &  Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh on 19th & 20th; East Uttar Pradesh, East Rajasthan, East  Madhya Pradesh during 19th-23rd;  Jharkhand  on  20th  &  21st;  West  Uttar  Pradesh  during  20th-22nd;  West  Rajasthan during 20th-23rd and Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Odisha during 21st-23rd April.
texts.text:  Heat  wave  conditions  very  likely  in  isolated  pockets  of  West  Madhya  Pradesh  &  Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh on 19th & 20th; East Uttar Pradesh, East Rajasthan, East  Madhya Pradesh during 19th-23rd;  Jharkhand  on  20th  &  21st;  West  Uttar  Pradesh  during  20th-22nd;  West  Rajasthan during 20th-23rd and Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Odisha during 21st-23rd April.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  16  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 78.481 billion cubic meter, which is 42.75% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 116.25 percent of last  year's  storage  and  127.20  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  16  April  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 78.481 billion cubic meter, which is 42.75% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 116.25 percent of last  year's  storage  and  127.20  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last

texts.label: text
texts.orig: year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (09 April - 15 April 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 46%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 15 April 2026) is higher than LPA by 14%.
texts.text:  During the week (09 April - 15 April 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 46%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 15 April 2026) is higher than LPA by 14%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall,  thunderstorm  activity  is  likely  over  the  northeast  &  adjoining  east  India  &  central  India during some days of the week.
texts.text: Overall,  thunderstorm  activity  is  likely  over  the  northeast  &  adjoining  east  India  &  central  India during some days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall is likely  to  be  normal to above  normal over many parts of northeast & adjoining East India, and near normal or dry weather is likely over the remaining parts of the country.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely  to  be  normal to above  normal over many parts of northeast & adjoining East India, and near normal or dry weather is likely over the remaining parts of the country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: 2. TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (17 April - 23 April 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (17 April - 23 April 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 2-4°C likely over Northwest India except East Uttar Pradesh where no significant change in maximum temperatures during next 7 days.
texts.text:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 2-4°C likely over Northwest India except East Uttar Pradesh where no significant change in maximum temperatures during next 7 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 2-3°C likely over East India during 20th-21st April and no significant change during 22nd -25th April.
texts.text:  Gradual rise in maximum temperatures by 2-3°C likely over East India during 20th-21st April and no significant change during 22nd -25th April.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over rest parts of the country during 20th to 25th April.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over rest parts of the country during 20th to 25th April.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (24 April - 30 April 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (24 April - 30 April 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low Probability of Heatwave conditions likely during some days of week 2 over central & adjoining south  peninsular  parts  of  the  country  (Madhya  Pradesh,  East  Rajasthan,  southern  parts  of  Uttar Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  &  Vidarbha).  Appreciably  above  normal  (+3.1  to  +5.0°C) maximum  temperatures  are  likely  over  some  parts  of  Jammu  &  Kashmir,  and  above-normal maximum temperatures (+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over most parts of Northwest & Central India, and many parts  of  the  East  &  Northeast,  and  the  West  coast  of  India.  Near-normal  weekly  maximum temperatures are likely in isolated places over the rest of the country.
texts.text:  Low Probability of Heatwave conditions likely during some days of week 2 over central & adjoining south  peninsular  parts  of  the  country  (Madhya  Pradesh,  East  Rajasthan,  southern  parts  of  Uttar Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  &  Vidarbha).  Appreciably  above  normal  (+3.1  to  +5.0°C) maximum  temperatures  are  likely  over  some  parts  of  Jammu  &  Kashmir,  and  above-normal maximum temperatures (+1.6 to +3.0°C) are likely over most parts of Northwest & Central India, and many parts  of  the  East  &  Northeast,  and  the  West  coast  of  India.  Near-normal  weekly  maximum temperatures are likely in isolated places over the rest of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rice : About 30.64 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.31 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice : About 30.64 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.31 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses :  About 15.47 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 13.49 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses :  About 15.47 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 13.49 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.orig: As on 16 April, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 78.481 BCM (42.75%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 16 April, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 78.481 BCM (42.75%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.orig:  Soil moisture during 10 April - 16 April 2026 is similar/better than the average of the past 10 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  Gujarat,  Maharashtra,  Telangana,  A.P.,  Karnataka,  Kerala,  Tamil  Nadu, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Uttarakhand, H.P., J & K, Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal and N.E. States.

States.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  It was lower than average of past 10 years in parts of Odisha, Chhattisgarh and Mizoram.
texts.text:  It was lower than average of past 10 years in parts of Odisha, Chhattisgarh and Mizoram.
